title: Dale Circle PASS: P=2W, Q=P, R=W² verified for 7 tuples
_fixture_purpose: Certifies Dale Pond's integer circle construction (svpwiki.com, 1998). Three new QA elements: P (diameter)=2W, Q (circumference)=P, R (area)=W². In QA circular units, pi disappears — diameter equals circumference. Extends Ben Iverson's 21 elements to 24 (with S from [190]).
source_attribution: Dale Pond, 'Unpublished Notes' / 'Quantum Arithmetic Elements', svpwiki.com, December 1998. Quote: 'When Ben said there was no way to define a circle with Quantum Arithmetic, I developed three different ways to do this.'

P.meaning: Circle diameter in QA integer units

Q.meaning: Circle circumference in QA integer units

R.meaning: Circle area in QA integer units

key_insight: Dale defines P=2W as diameter, Q=P as circumference, R=W² as area — effectively choosing QA circular units where pi=1. This is a unit convention (analogous to natural units c=1 in physics), not a structural theorem. The algebraic identities P=2W, Q=P, R=W² are exact by definition. Dale's contribution is identifying W as the natural radius-like quantity for QA circles.

witnesses.note: Unity Block: W=8 (satellite period), P=Q=16, R=64=8²
Female Unity: W=15, P=Q=30 (3D QN requirement), R=225

cross_references.name: QA Equilateral Triangle Cert
cross_references.relation: W = equilateral side; P,Q,R derived from W

cross_references.name: QA Eisenstein Norm Cert
cross_references.relation: F²-FW+W²=Z²; W is the Eisenstein equilateral side

cross_references.name: QA Equilateral Height Cert
cross_references.relation: S = equilateral height; S,W,P form equilateral-circle bridge
